subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Set Contents
- **Investigator cards** — 5 investigators with unique abilities, stats, and signature cards.
- **Player cards** — ~100 cards across 5 classes (Guardian, Seeker, Rogue, Mystic, Survivor) plus Neutral.
- **Scenario cards** — 3 scenarios forming "Night of the Zealot" campaign.
- **Act deck cards** — Goals players must complete.
- **Agenda deck cards** — Doom clock threat that advances bad events.
- **Location cards** — Double-sided cards representing explorable locations.
- **Enemy cards** — Monsters and cultists.
- **Treachery cards** — Encounter deck hazards.
- **Chaos tokens** — 16 tokens drawn from chaos bag for skill tests.
- **Resource tokens** — Currency for playing cards.
- **Clue/doom tokens** — Dual-sided tokens (clue on one side, doom on other).
- **Horror/damage tokens** — Track mental and physical harm.
- **Mini cards** — Investigator mini-cards for tracking location.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Select scenario** — Choose the scenario from the campaign being played.
2. **Build chaos bag** — Add tokens matching the chosen difficulty (Easy, Standard, Hard, Expert).
3. **Set aside act deck** — Place act cards in order, act 1 on top.
4. **Set aside agenda deck** — Place agenda cards in order, agenda 1 on top.
5. **Place starting location(s)** — Put the scenario's initial location(s) face up.
6. **Build encounter deck** — Combine scenario encounter sets as instructed; shuffle.
7. **Place doom on agenda** — Some scenarios start with doom; follow instructions.
8. **Set aside special cards** — Some enemies, locations, or story assets start set aside.
9. **Supply tokens** — Place resource, clue, doom, damage, and horror tokens in shared pools.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Deck Construction
- **Deck size** — Each investigator requires exactly 30 cards (excluding signature cards and weaknesses).
- **Class restriction** — Each investigator specifies which class(es) they can include.
- **Level restriction** — At campaign start, only level 0 cards are allowed.
- **Signature cards** — Add investigator's required signature asset(s) and weakness(es) to deck.
- **Basic weakness** — Draw 1 random basic weakness and add it to deck.

#### At the Table
- **Place investigator card** — Set investigator card face up with full health and sanity.
- **Set resources** — Take 5 resources.
- **Draw opening hand** — Draw 5 cards.
- **Mulligan** — You may set aside any number of cards, draw replacements, then shuffle set-aside cards back.
- **Place mini-card** — Put investigator mini-card at starting location.

--- Solo (1 Player) ---
- **Single investigator** — Play one investigator; some scenarios are harder solo.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Standard play** — Each player builds a separate deck; recommended starting configuration.

--- 3-4 Players ---
- **Two core sets recommended** — A single core set has limited copies of key player cards.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Read scenario intro** — Read the campaign/scenario flavor text aloud.
2. **Place investigators** — All investigators start at the designated starting location.
3. **Collect clues** — Place clue tokens on locations equal to their clue value (per investigator scaling).
4. **Lead investigator** — Choose one player as lead investigator for resolving ties and decisions.
5. **Begin first round** — Start with the Mythos phase (skipped on round 1 in most scenarios) or Investigation phase as scenario directs.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Phases (in order)
1. **Mythos Phase** — (Skipped on round 1.) Advance doom, check agenda threshold, draw 1 encounter card per investigator.
2. **Investigation Phase** — Each investigator takes a turn with 3 actions.
3. **Enemy Phase** — Engaged enemies attack (deal damage and/or horror).
4. **Upkeep Phase** — Ready all exhausted cards, draw 1 card, gain 1 resource.

#### Mythos Phase Detail
- **Place doom** — Add 1 doom to current agenda.
- **Check doom threshold** — If total doom on agenda + all cards in play meets/exceeds threshold, advance agenda (bad things happen).
- **Draw encounter cards** — Each investigator draws 1 encounter card and resolves it (enemies engage, treacheries resolve).

#### Investigation Phase Detail
- **Turn order** — Lead investigator chooses turn order each round.
- **3 actions per turn** — Each investigator gets 3 actions on their turn.
- **Free actions** — Some card abilities grant free actions (lightning bolt icon) that do not count toward the 3-action limit.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Basic Actions (cost 1 action each)
- **Draw** — Draw 1 card from your deck.
- **Resource** — Gain 1 resource.
- **Play a card** — Play an asset or event from hand, paying its resource cost.
- **Activate** — Use an action ability on a card in play (arrow icon).
- **Investigate** — Test intellect against location's shroud value; if successful, discover 1 clue.
- **Move** — Move to a connected location.
- **Engage** — Engage an enemy at your location (pull it to you from another investigator).
- **Evade** — Test agility vs enemy's evade value; if successful, exhaust and disengage from the enemy.
- **Fight** — Test combat vs enemy's fight value; if successful, deal 1 damage (or weapon's damage).

#### Skill Tests
1. **Determine skill and difficulty** — Identify which skill (willpower, intellect, combat, agility) and the test's difficulty value.
2. **Commit cards** — Investigators at the same location may commit cards with matching skill icons.
3. **Draw chaos token** — Draw 1 token from the chaos bag and apply its modifier.
4. **Determine success** — Skill value + committed icons + token modifier >= difficulty = success.
5. **Apply results** — Resolve success/failure effects.

#### Chaos Token Types
- **Number tokens** — Apply as modifier (+1, 0, -1, -2, etc.).
- **Elder Sign** — Investigator's personal special effect (usually positive).
- **Skull, Cultist, Tablet, Elder Thing** — Scenario-specific negative effects.
- **Auto-fail** — Automatic failure regardless of skill value.
- **Blessing/Curse** — (With expansions) draw again with +2 or -2.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Doom Clock
- **Doom threshold** — Each agenda card has a doom threshold printed on it.
- **Doom accumulation** — 1 doom is added per round, plus card effects may add more.
- **Agenda advance** — When total doom meets threshold, the agenda advances: remove all doom, flip agenda, resolve its effects (often very bad).
- **Campaign pressure** — The doom clock creates urgency to complete act objectives before the agenda overwhelms you.

#### Act Deck Progression
- **Clue spending** — Most acts advance by spending collected clues (usually scaled by investigator count).
- **Act advance** — When conditions are met, advance the act (good progress) by flipping or replacing the act card.

#### Damage & Horror
- **Health** — Each investigator has a health value; damage tokens track physical harm.
- **Sanity** — Each investigator has a sanity value; horror tokens track mental harm.
- **Defeat** — If damage equals health or horror equals sanity, the investigator is defeated.
- **Allies absorb** — Ally assets can soak damage and horror up to their printed values.

#### Engagement & Enemies
- **Hunter enemies** — Move toward nearest investigator during enemy phase.
- **Engaged enemies** — Attack the engaged investigator during enemy phase.
- **Aloof enemies** — Do not automatically engage; must be engaged manually.
- **Attack of opportunity** — Taking non-fight, non-evade actions while engaged provokes enemy attacks.

#### Campaign Mechanics
- **XP rewards** — Earn XP at scenario end based on victory display cards and scenario resolution.
- **Deck upgrades** — Spend XP to add higher-level cards or swap cards between scenarios.
- **Trauma** — Defeated investigators gain physical or mental trauma, starting subsequent scenarios with that much damage/horror.
- **Resolution** — Different scenario outcomes lead to different campaign branches and narrative consequences.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Scenario Resolution
- **Win by completing acts** — Advance through all act cards to reach a successful resolution.
- **Lose by agenda** — If the agenda advances past its final card, a negative resolution occurs.
- **Investigator defeat** — Defeated investigators are eliminated for the remainder of the scenario.
- **Resolution text** — Read the matching resolution entry in the campaign guide.

#### Campaign Scoring
- **XP earned** — Sum victory points of defeated enemies/locations in the victory display, plus scenario bonus XP.
- **Trauma** — Record any physical or mental trauma for each investigator.
- **Campaign log** — Note story decisions, allies gained, enemies made.
- **Final scenario** — The last scenario of a campaign determines overall success or failure.

#### No VP Tally
- **Cooperative outcome** — There is no competitive score; the team either succeeds or fails each scenario.
- **Campaign arc** — Success is measured by surviving the full campaign and achieving the best possible resolution.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Mulligan aggressively** — Look for key assets (weapons for Guardians, investigation tools for Seekers) in your opening hand.
- **Play assets early** — Spend early actions setting up crucial assets before the encounter deck ramps up.
- **Split responsibilities** — Assign clue-gathering to high-intellect investigators and combat to high-combat investigators.
- **Manage the doom clock** — Count remaining rounds before the agenda advances and plan accordingly.
- **Save resources for critical tests** — Do not over-commit resources early; the hardest tests come later.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring the doom clock** — Spending too many actions drawing cards or gaining resources while doom accumulates.
- **Over-splitting the party** — Spreading investigators across too many locations leaves everyone vulnerable.
- **Forgetting attacks of opportunity** — Taking non-combat actions while engaged with enemies provokes free attacks.
- **Hoarding cards for skill tests** — Committing too few cards to critical tests leads to wasted actions on failures.
- **Neglecting soak assets** — Allies that absorb damage and horror are essential for survival, not optional.
- **Poor XP spending** — Upgrading flashy but situational cards instead of reliable core upgrades.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Fight vs evade** — Decide whether to spend actions killing an enemy or evading to buy time for clue gathering.
- **Commit vs hold** — Weigh committing skill cards now for a sure pass against saving them for future critical tests.
- **Rush vs setup** — Balance spending early turns playing assets against immediately pursuing scenario objectives.
- **Risk the chaos bag** — Determine if your skill value gives acceptable odds or if you need to commit more icons.
- **Resign vs push forward** — Some scenarios allow resignation; weigh partial credit against risking defeat and trauma.
